title: hippocrepis scabra subsp. commutata
intro: Hippocrepis scabra subsp. commutata, also known as the hairy broom-spurrey, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Hippocrepis scabra subsp. commutata is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
